documents.title: Tulane University public AI hub or guidance

ai_strategy.summary: Tulane University's public AI posture is visible through governance guidance, teaching resources, research activity, and enterprise/tool support. The evidence points to a pragmatic R1 approach: encourage AI literacy and research while constraining sensitive data and academic-integrity risks.

key_provisions: Use AI in ways consistent with academic integrity and existing university policy.
Protect sensitive, confidential, student, research, or regulated data when using AI tools.
Faculty or academic units retain responsibility for clear course-level expectations where applicable.
Human review and accountability remain necessary for AI-assisted work.
ai_policy.summary: Tulane University has public AI guidance or policy-adjacent materials. The cited guidance addresses responsible use, data protection, ethics, and course or work-context expectations.

bodies.name: University committees on GenAI in classroom and research
bodies.scope: Coordinates or evidences institutional AI governance, guidance, research, teaching, or tool enablement.

governance_structure.summary: Public evidence identifies University committees on GenAI in classroom and research as the main AI governance or coordination signal for Tulane University.

initiatives.title: University committees on GenAI in classroom and research AI governance and guidance
initiatives.description: Tulane University publishes AI guidance or governance resources that frame responsible use, academic integrity, privacy, and institutional oversight. The cited materials make AI a university-level issue rather than only a classroom technology topic.
initiatives.significance: This is a material public signal of how an R1 university is translating AI from discussion into institutional practice.

investment.description: No public dollar amount was identified for this specific initiative.

collaborations.description: No external collaboration was identified in the cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: The public guidance creates an institutional baseline for course, research, and administrative AI use.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Tulane University responsible AI and access signal
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Cited public materials connect AI adoption to privacy, bias, academic integrity, access, community benefit, or responsible human use.

regulatory_context.summary: Louisiana public AI activity is relevant context; this profile treats campus policy and institutional evidence as the primary governance layer.

narrative_summary: Tulane University presents an AI posture typical of an active R1 institution: public guidance for generative AI, visible teaching resources, research activity, and a growing set of supported tools or infrastructure. The most mature signals are governance and responsible-use guidance, teaching and syllabus support, and research capacity through Jurist Center for Artificial Intelligence and Center for Community-Engaged AI. Artificial Intelligence minor gives the institution a workforce-development or curriculum signal, while Campus AI guidance and data-classification rules shows that AI is also being handled as an enterprise technology and data-governance issue. Public sources also show attention to responsible use, access, bias, privacy, or community impact, though some operational details remain incomplete. In particular, current public pages do not consistently disclose named executive ownership, all procurement decisions, full user counts, or complete dollar amounts. This profile therefore treats the institution as operational and growing rather than fully transformative: AI is embedded across policy, pedagogy, research, and tools, but not every part of the campus AI operating model is publicly documented.

research_quality.compiled_by: codex / collect-institution skill / Worker 1 gap sweep
search_queries: "Tulane University" artificial intelligence AI policy generative AI
"Tulane University" AI research center institute machine learning
Tulane Microsoft Copilot AI tools faculty students
Tulane generative AI teaching assessment syllabus
Tulane high performance computing cluster GPU artificial intelligence
Tulane artificial intelligence grant funding center institute
gaps_acknowledged: Leadership names, enrollment, and exact enterprise AI user counts were not filled unless directly necessary; public pages were stronger for initiatives than current-role rosters.
Some initiatives cite policy or hub pages because a single dated strategy document was not publicly identified.
No comprehensive AI budget was identified beyond explicitly cited grants or public funding signals.
Follow-up search closed platform_usage_or_contracts at product/scope level with Copilot for all faculty, staff and students, but no Tulane user count or license cost was found.
Follow-up search found Cypress HPC and up-to-$10,000 Data/AI/Community grants; no public GPU count or aggregate AI investment total was found.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
